app.name: Teal
app.bestFor: Most complete all-in-one job hunt: resume builder plus a job tracker and keyword match scoring against listings.

app.name: Rezi
app.bestFor: Deepest ATS-optimization focus, built around an internal ATS scoring system; a Reddit favorite for beating filters.

app.name: Simplify
app.bestFor: Chrome extension that autofills applications across Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Workday and LinkedIn (you still hit submit).

app.name: Kickresume
app.bestFor: Strongest AI first drafts for summaries and bullet points, with a large polished template library.

app.name: Zety
app.bestFor: Cleanest template output and guided step-by-step building for a fast, polished-looking resume.

app.name: Sheets Resume
app.bestFor: Simple Google-Docs-style approach from an ex-recruiter; favored on Reddit for clean, no-nonsense resumes.

skill.name: Reactive-Resume
skill.bestFor: The gold-standard open-source resume builder — privacy-first, self-hostable, dozens of templates, one-click PDF export. The recognized category leader (~40k stars).

skill.name: Awesome-CV
skill.bestFor: The iconic LaTeX résumé/CV template used across tech — clean, ATS-parseable typography (~28k stars).

skill.name: RenderCV
skill.bestFor: YAML-to-PDF résumé engine with version-controllable source and ATS-friendly output; hugely popular with engineers (~17k stars).

skill.name: best-resume-ever (salomonelli)
skill.bestFor: Vue-based builder that generates multiple polished résumé designs from one data file (~16k stars).

skill.name: OpenResume
skill.bestFor: Open-source résumé builder and parser focused on ATS compatibility; runs fully in your browser, no signup (~9k stars).

skill.name: sb2nov/resume
skill.bestFor: Widely-forked LaTeX software-engineer résumé template; a de-facto GitHub starting point (~7k stars).

skill.name: JSON Resume (resume-cli)
skill.bestFor: Official CLI for the JSON Resume standard — one JSON file, many community themes and export formats (~5k stars).

skill.name: ResumeSkills (Paramchoudhary)
skill.bestFor: The most complete Agent Skills pack for job seekers: 20 SKILL.md skills covering ATS optimization, bullet writing, job-fit analysis, and interview prep (~1.3k stars).

skill.name: resume-cover-letter (jezweb/claude-skills)
skill.bestFor: Resume + cover-letter SKILL.md inside a popular general Claude Code skills/plugins collection (~940 stars overall).

skill.name: proficiently-claude-skills (proficientlyjobs)
skill.bestFor: Claude Code skills for AI-powered job search, resume tailoring, and cover-letter writing with a structured per-application workflow (~300 stars).

notes: App track has strong, Reddit-corroborated consensus (Teal, Rezi, Simplify). Note that mass auto-apply bots like LazyApply are widely criticized (low Trustpilot scores, LinkedIn now flags 'human-impossible' velocity), so autofill-with-review tools (Simplify) are ranked over spray-and-pray automation. The Skill/Repo track is now ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption (GitHub stars) rather than form factor — kind ('skill' vs 'repo') is only a tag. It mixes the notable open-source résumé repos (Reactive-Resume, Awesome-CV, RenderCV, best-resume-ever, OpenResume, sb2nov, JSON Resume) with the leading resume Agent Skills; Paramchoudhary's ResumeSkills is the strongest dedicated skill but now ranks below the far more adopted builder repos. Star counts verified via the GitHub API (Jul 2026); the tiny jananthan30/Resume-Builder (~64 stars) was dropped as below the quality bar.
